ksa_text: Skill in evaluating AI bias and fairness, including designing and executing fairness metrics, detecting and measuring bias throughout the AI lifecycle, and conducting privacy risk assessment.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although AI Governance is an edge domain for the Director of Data Operations, this skill KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.536) and conceptual alignment to include at the director level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.536). External reviewers may question whether this specific skill KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Operations versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act (Article 9), and OECD AI Principles confirm AI governance as a critical competency for responsible AI leadership.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with AI Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Data Operations.

decision_rationale: Edge-domain AI Governance skill KSA cleared adversarial review with score 0.536. Included based on sufficient framework alignment and role relevance for the Director of Data Operations.
